title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Christine Redfern describes the deceased Eleanor Stewart as a useless, unintelligent parasite who was frequently involved in "dirty affairs" such as extortion and jealousy. Colonel Weston questions Redfern on her use of the word "extortion," leading her to admit that she accidentally overheard a man with a muffled, unrecognizable voice extorting Mrs. Marshall three days prior while playing bridge.

Following Redfern's departure, Inspector Cargraves, Colonel Weston, Poirot, and Detective Inspector Colgate discuss the possibility that Mrs. Marshall was meeting a blackmailer at Fairy Bay. They note that the location is ideal for a secret meeting because it is deserted, accessible only by a vertical steel ladder, and contains a hidden cave known as the Fairy Cave. Weston narrows the potential suspects staying at the hotel to Mr. Gardner, Major Barry, Mr. Horace Blatt, and Reverend Stephen Lane, noting that the latter three were out during the morning.

Colonel Weston summons the American couple, the Gardners, for questioning. Mrs. Gardner claims she and her husband were on the beach all morning. She expresses her disdain for Mrs. Marshall, calling her dim-witted and uncivilized, and suggests that Mr. Marshall should have married the intelligent and successful Rosamond Dunnely instead. She further remarks that everyone could see how much Dunnely loved Mr. Marshall.
